article_meta.title: New York Playbook POLITICO's must-read briefing informing the daily conversation among knowledgeable New Yorkers New Yor

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for a lengthy newsletter-style article. This is a Politico Playbook briefing, which is inherently a mix of news aggregation, commentary, and newsletter-style prose. There are some defensible bias instances in the author's prose, though many are borderline given the newsletter format. Key potential instances: (1) 'evading the prohibition against lobbyist donating' - the word 'evading' implies intentional circumvention, which is author's loaded word choice; (2) 'pulled its punches with him' regarding NY Post - author's editorial characterization; (3) 'MAGA minions' appears in Avlon's quote so correctly excluded; (4) 'Avlon's history isn't true blue though' - author's own editorial commentary/spin; (5) 'was roundly mocked on social media' - author's characterization. The model missed some defensible instances but many are borderline in a newsletter format where some editorial voice is expected.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible author-level bias instances: 'evading the prohibition' (Word Choice - loaded verb implying intentional wrongdoing), 'Avlon's history isn't true blue though' (Spin/editorial commentary), 'pulled its punches with him' (Word Choice/Spin), 'was roundly mocked' (Subjective Qualifying Adjective). Some of these are moderate instances given the newsletter format, but 'evading' and 'isn't true blue' are clear editorial choices worth flagging.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No items were flagged so no type labels to evaluate.
